purpose: Educational report on cryptojacking threats and defensive measures
disclaimer: FOR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY - NO ACTUAL ATTACKS OCCUR

browser_mining.description: Malicious JavaScript code that uses visitors' CPU power to mine cryptocurrencies without their knowledge.
indicators: High CPU usage in browser processes
browser_mining.impact: Reduced system performance, increased electricity costs, potential data theft
mitigation_strategies: Use ad blockers and script blockers

malware_mining.description: Malicious software that installs mining programs on infected systems without user consent.
malware_mining.impact: System performance degradation, increased electricity costs, potential data theft, hardware damage

container_mining.description: Malicious containers that run mining operations in containerized environments.
container_mining.impact: Resource exhaustion, increased cloud costs, potential data breach, service degradation

edr.description: Security tools that monitor and respond to threats on endpoints in real-time.
detection_rules: High CPU usage by unknown processes
educational_notes: EDR tools provide comprehensive endpoint visibility
Real-time monitoring is essential for threat detection
Custom rules can be created for specific threats
Integration with other security tools enhances detection

antivirus.description: Software that detects and removes malicious programs from systems.
educational_notes: Antivirus software is essential but not sufficient
Regular updates are crucial for effectiveness
False positives may occur with legitimate mining software

ids.description: Network security tools that monitor network traffic for suspicious activity.
educational_notes: Protocol analysis can identify mining traffic
Integration with other tools enhances detection

firewall.description: Network security devices that control incoming and outgoing network traffic.
educational_notes: Application control can prevent mining software execution
Integration with other security tools enhances protection

items: Use strong, unique passwords for all accounts
Use dedicated accounts for different purposes

suspicious_processes.description: Detect processes that may be related to mining activity
suspicious_processes.query_syntax:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4688 
                | search "xmrig" OR "miner" OR "crypto" OR "hash"
                | table _time host user process_name command_line
suspicious_processes.educational_purpose: Identify processes that may be mining software
suspicious_processes.expected_results: List of processes with mining-related names or commands

high_cpu_usage.description: Detect processes with unusually high CPU usage
high_cpu_usage.query_syntax:
index=performance sourcetype=perfmon:Processor 
                | where CPU_Usage > 80
                | join type=inner host, _time 
                [search index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4688]
                | table _time host process_name CPU_Usage
high_cpu_usage.educational_purpose: Identify processes consuming excessive CPU resources
high_cpu_usage.expected_results: List of processes with high CPU usage over time

mining_pool_connections.description: Detect network connections to known mining pools
mining_pool_connections.query_syntax:
index=network sourcetype=firewall 
                | search "pool" OR "stratum" OR "3333" OR "14444"
                | lookup mining_pools.csv pool_address OUTPUT pool_name
                | table _time src_ip dest_ip dest_port pool_name
mining_pool_connections.educational_purpose: Identify connections to mining pool servers
mining_pool_connections.expected_results: List of network connections to mining pools

suspicious_registry.description: Detect registry modifications that may indicate mining software installation
suspicious_registry.query_syntax:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4657
                | search "miner" OR "xmrig" OR "crypto"
                | table _time host user registry_key new_value
suspicious_registry.educational_purpose: Identify registry changes related to mining software
suspicious_registry.expected_results: List of registry modifications with mining-related content

yara_rules.mining_software:
rule Mining_Software_Detection {
    meta:
        description = "Detect common mining software"
        author = "Educational Example"
        date = "2024"
        purpose = "Educational - for defensive analysis only"
    
    strings:
        $xmrig = "xmrig" nocase
        $t_rex = "t-rex" nocase
        $teamred = "teamredminer" nocase
        $mining = "mining" nocase
        $stratum = "stratum" nocase
        $pool = "pool" nocase
    
    condition:
        any of ($xmrig, $t_rex, $teamred, $mining, $stratum, $pool)
}
yara_rules.cryptojacking_indicators:
rule Cryptojacking_Indicators {
    meta:
        description = "Detect cryptojacking indicators"
        author = "Educational Example"
        date = "2024"
        purpose = "Educational - for defensive analysis only"
    
    strings:
        $coinhive = "coinhive" nocase
        $cryptoloot = "cryptoloot" nocase
        $webminer = "webminer" nocase
        $mining_js = "mining.js" nocase
        $worker_js = "worker.js" nocase
    
    condition:
        any of ($coinhive, $cryptoloot, $webminer, $mining_js, $worker_js)
}

sigma_rules.high_cpu_usage:
title: High CPU Usage Detection
id: high-cpu-usage-001
description: Detect processes with unusually high CPU usage
author: Educational Example
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
    - attack.defense_evasion
    - attack.execution
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4688
        ProcessName:
            - 'xmrig.exe'
            - 't-rex.exe'
            - 'teamredminer.exe'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate mining software (if authorized)
level: medium
sigma_rules.mining_pool_connections:
title: Mining Pool Connection Detection
id: mining-pool-connection-001
description: Detect connections to known mining pools
author: Educational Example
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
    - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5156
        DestinationPort:
            - 3333
            - 14444
            - 10032
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate mining operations (if authorized)
level: high

detection: Monitor system performance for unusual CPU usage
Check for unknown processes in task manager
Review network connections for mining pool traffic
Analyze system logs for suspicious activity
eradication: Remove mining software and related files

educational_objectives: Understand cryptojacking threats and attack vectors
Learn about security tools and their configurations
Implement security best practices and checklists
Develop log analysis and threat detection skills
Build defensive capabilities against mining threats
